profile.primary_purpose: Convert visitors to paid subscribers by presenting transparent, tiered pricing options for individuals and teams
profile.brand_signature: Dark, developer-focused interface with a near-black background (#121212) and warm off-white text. Clean, utilitarian aesthetic featuring card-based pricing tiers with subtle surface elevation and minimal decorative elements, anchored by a purple accent for the recommended tier.

visual_language.accent_strategy: Purple/violet accent (#71.5% oklch) used sparingly to highlight the recommended 'Build' tier and tertiary elements, creating visual hierarchy against the neutral dark palette.
visual_language.highlights: Animated gradient border on the recommended pricing card draws attention; subtle surface elevation differentiates pricing tiers

target_audience: Individual developers seeking a modern terminal with AI capabilities
Development teams evaluating productivity tools with collaboration features
Enterprise engineering leaders requiring security, compliance, and admin controls
strategy.executive_synthesis: Warp's pricing page leverages a dark, developer-native visual identity that immediately signals technical credibility. The clean card-based tier system with transparent pricing ($0 to Custom) creates a clear upgrade path. The purple-highlighted 'Build' tier with animated border strategically draws attention to the recommended entry point, while extensive feature tooltips and FAQ sections address technical buyer concerns. Enterprise positioning with SAML SSO, admin controls, and 'bring your own LLM' options targets larger organizations. Social proof via logo bar reinforces adoption by recognized tech companies.

style_classification.reasoning: The visual impression from the screenshot reveals a dark-mode SaaS pricing page with clean, structured layouts and restrained decoration. The design exhibits hallmarks of Flat Design: geometric shapes (rectangular pricing cards), bold saturated accent colors (purple #CBB0F7 against monochromatic black/gray surfaces), sans-serif modern typography (Inter for body, Matter for display), and a strict 12-column grid system with generous whitespace. The texture profile confirms "shadows_soft" and "glass_blur" effects, but these are subtle enhancements rather than defining characteristics—the overall aesthetic remains fundamentally flat with minimal depth. The color palette is primarily monochromatic (deep blacks #121212, charcoal grays #1B1A18, whites #F8F5EE) punctuated by a vibrant purple accent, aligning with Flat Design's use of bold, saturated colors against clean backgrounds. The card-based layout with clear separation, systematic comparison tables, and feature checkmarks all reinforce the digital-native, 2D clarity central to Flat Design. The layout is balanced (not dense, not sparse), dimensionality is decidedly flat (2D), decoration is minimal, and structure is rigid/grid-based.
style_classification.reasoning_secondary: The Corporate modifier applies strongly here due to the design's professional, trust-building intent. This is a B2B/developer tool pricing page featuring: enterprise tier offerings, partner logo social proof strip (VMware, Vercel, DoorDash, GitHub), detailed feature comparison matrices, FAQ accordion for transparency, and multi-platform download options. The typography hierarchy (Matter for headings, Inter for body, DM Mono for technical labels) creates a systematic, professional voice. The restrained color usage (single purple accent against neutral darks), conventional pricing card layouts, and emphasis on feature comprehensiveness over visual flair all signal corporate reliability over creative experimentation. The design prioritizes clarity, information hierarchy, and conversion optimization—hallmarks of Corporate styling within a Flat Design foundation.

layout.section_separation: Sections are separated by generous vertical padding (var(--space-section) which equals calc(4rem * var(--density))). Background alternates between solid black and subtle dark gray surface colors. Some sections use visual breaks like the logo strip marquee and decorative gradient image. The comparison table has subtle grid lines and borders. No structural divider lines are used; separation is achieved through whitespace and background color contrast.

---VISUAL ANNOTATIONS---

**NAV:**
- Background: Solid black
- Logo: "Warp" wordmark in white
- Nav links: White text, minimal spacing
- Buttons/CTAs: "Log in" (text link, white) | "Sign up" (solid white button, rounded)
- Colors: Black background, white text/elements

**HERO:**
- Background: Black
- Typography: Large "Pricing" headline in white
- Subtext: Gray text about scaling
- Toggle switch: "Monthly"/"Yearly" with purple accent
- Colors: Black, white, gray, purple accent

**PRICING TIERS (3-column):**
- Background: Dark gray/black cards with subtle borders
- Cards: Three equal-width pricing cards with rounded corners
- Middle card: Purple "POPULAR" badge
- Buttons/CTAs: 
  - "Sign up" (outline, white border on Free tier)
  - "Start trial →" (solid purple gradient, Draft tier)
  - "Contact us" (outline, white border on Max tier)
- Typography: Large white price numbers, gray descriptive text
- Feature lists: Checkmarks in subtle gray
- Colors: Black cards, white text, purple accent on middle card

**ADDITIONAL TIERS (2-column):**
- Background: Same dark card treatment
- Buttons/CTAs:
  - "Contact us" (outline button on Business)
  - "Contact sales" (outline button on Enterprise)
- Layout: Two equal-width cards, more spacious
- Colors: Consistent black/white/gray scheme

**PARTNER LOGOS:**
- Background: Black
- Logos: Grayscale/white company logos in horizontal row
- Style: Muted, desaturated treatment

**COMPARISON TABLE:**
- Background: Dark with subtle grid lines
- Headers: Column headers for each pricing tier
- Rows: Feature categories with checkmarks (✓) in purple/white
- Typography: Small, readable sans-serif
- Interactive: Expandable category sections
- Colors: Dark gray background, purple checkmarks

**TRANSPARENCY SECTION:**
- Background: Black
- Headline: Large white text, centered
- Buttons/CTAs: "Learn more" and "FAQ" (both text links or subtle buttons)
- Cards: Three feature cards with icons, dark background, subtle borders
- Colors: Black background, white text, blue/purple accent colors

**PRICING FAQ:**
- Background: Black
- Accordion items: Each question row with [+] expand icon on right
- Typography: White question text, subtle divider lines
- Interactive: Expandable/collapsible items
- Colors: Black, white text, gray dividers

**GRADIENT IMAGE:**
- Background: Large abstract gradient (teal/blue/purple flowing colors)
- Style: Soft, blurred, atmospheric
- Purpose: Visual break, adds color to dark page

**GET WARP TODAY:**
- Background: Black
- Headline: White text
- Platform icons: macOS, Linux, Windows with respective icons
- Buttons/CTAs:
  - "macOS" → "Intel" | "Apple Silicon" (white outline buttons)
  - "Linux" → ".deb" | other options (white outline buttons)
  - "Windows" → ".exe" (white outline button)
- Layout: Grid of download options
- Colors: Black background, white buttons and text

**FOOTER:**
- Background: Solid black
- Layout: 5-column link grid
- Typography: Small gray text for links, white for headings
- Social icons: White/gray
- Bottom bar: Copyright text and language selector
- Colors: Black background, gray/white text

rules.description: Deep background defaults used heavily for app canvas and structural wrapping elements.
Elevated dark values used above the global background for cards, popovers, and segmented containers.
Mid-level grays for dividers, silent icon details, and non-distracting borders.
High-contrast bright values primarily for primary text, active states, buttons, and prominent typography.
Secondary text rendering for descriptions, hints, and slightly lowered typographic hierarchy.
Neutral warming base for slightly desaturated greenish gray instances. Used mostly as contextual token values.
Primary brand hue and agentic AI accent color, signifying orchestration, magic, and dynamic actions.
Success, verification, and green-themed component branding.
Information, subtle actions, and cool terminal thematic aesthetics.
Destructive actions, error states, and high heat alerts.
Warning indicators and vibrant energetic branding moments.

insights: A dark-mode-first color palette relying on deep blacks and charcoal grays for backgrounds, panels, and borders to create spatial hierarchy.
Subtle muted grays are used for secondary typography and structural borders while high-contrast whites and light beiges draw focus to primary content.
Accent colors span a vibrant rainbow (purple, green, blue, red, yellow), acting as status cues, semantic highlights, and brand emphasis throughout the UI.

paragraphs.description: Body text sizes for descriptions and functional lists.

labels.description: Badges, categories, UI tabs, and strict microcopy.

others.description: Various display pricing metrics and large scale labels.

typography.highlights: Dotted underlines (decoration-dotted underline-offset-4) reveal additional functionality softly, while UI focus elements are marked natively with ring offsets.
insights: Strategic Fluidity: Headings scale seamlessly via `clamp()` functions tied to CSS variables (`clamp(2.5rem, 5vw, var(--heading-size))`), ensuring dynamic responsiveness without sudden breakpoint scaling.
Developer Persona: Heavy integration of 'DM Mono' for categorization and explicit keyboard shortcut styling (`<kbd>`) reinforces the technical, terminal-focused tooling identity of the brand.

Hierarchical Contrast: The custom geometric sans 'Matter' is exclusively assigned to Display headings, creating strong visual hierarchy when juxtaposed against the structured, highly-tracked 'Inter' utility blocks.

gradients.css_code: linear-gradient(to bottom in oklab, var(--tw-gradient-from) var(--tw-gradient-from-position), var(--tw-gradient-to) var(--tw-gradient-to-position))

gradients.name: Background Fade Bottom Right
gradients.description: Diagonal gradient from background color to transparent
gradients.css_code: linear-gradient(to bottom right in oklab, var(--tw-gradient-from) var(--tw-gradient-from-position), var(--tw-gradient-to) var(--tw-gradient-to-position))

gradients.name: Background with Via Transparent
gradients.description: Background color fading through transparent to another color
gradients.css_code: linear-gradient(to bottom in oklab, var(--color-background) var(--tw-gradient-from-position), transparent var(--tw-gradient-via-position), var(--tw-gradient-to) var(--tw-gradient-to-position))

gradients.css_code: linear-gradient(to bottom in oklab, rgba(12, 12, 9, 0.7) 0%, rgba(12, 12, 9, 0.55) 50%, rgba(12, 12, 9, 0.9) 100%)

gradients.name: Black Fade Overlay
gradients.description: Black gradient fading from 25% opacity to transparent
gradients.css_code: linear-gradient(to bottom in oklab, rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.25) 0%, transparent 100%)

gradients.name: Text Subtle Fade
gradients.description: Very subtle text color gradient from 5% to 10% opacity
gradients.css_code: linear-gradient(to bottom in oklab, color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-text) 5%, transparent) 0%, color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-text) 10%, transparent) 100%)

gradients.name: Animated Border Gradient
gradients.description: Rotating conic gradient for animated borders on featured pricing cards

highlights: Backdrop blur effects on navigation header: backdrop-blur-md (12px blur)
Glass panel navigation flyouts with bg-[#0C0C0C] and shadow-lg
Soft shadow system using shadow-sm, shadow-md, shadow-lg, and shadow-xl with subtle color-mixed black shadows
Animated spinning border gradient on featured 'Build' pricing card using conic gradient
Grayscale filter on logo carousel with opacity transitions on hover
Horizontal scrolling mask gradient effect on logo carousel: linear-gradient(to right, transparent, black 10%, black 90%, transparent)
Ring-based borders using inset-ring-1 with color-mixed border colors at various opacities
Subtle surface texture treatment with --surface-texture variable (currently set to none)
Color-mixed background overlays at multiple opacity levels (5%, 10%, 15%, 20%, etc.)
Shadow treatment on cards with transition-[box-shadow] duration-[var(--duration-normal)]

elevation.description: Elevation allows elements to be lifted from the background. It can be defined by applying drop shadows or other visual cues, such as color and borders

shadows.description: Elevation using shadows reflects a spatial relationship by indicating separation from the background or allowing content to be scrolled under another element.

levels.name: Tw Ring Shadow
levels.description: Tailwind ring shadow reset - transparent placeholder shadow used for ring offset calculations

levels.name: Img Shadow
levels.description: Image shadow with 8px vertical offset, 30px blur, and 40% black opacity for prominent elevation

insights: The design system uses CSS custom properties extensively (--color-*, --radius-*, --btn-*) allowing for easy theming and consistent token usage across components.
Navigation employs a glassmorphism effect with backdrop-blur-md and semi-transparent background (60-70% opacity) creating depth while maintaining content readability.
The pricing page features an animated gradient border on the recommended tier card using a slow-spinning pseudo-element, drawing attention without being distracting.
Button states follow a consistent pattern: solid fill for primary CTAs, ghost/transparent for secondary navigation, and outline with hover border-color changes for tertiary actions.
